person.name: Tokugawa Ieyasu
person.description: Tokugawa Ieyasu[a][b] (born Matsudaira Takechiyo;[c][d] January 31, 1543 – June 1, 1616) was a Japanese samurai, daimyo and the founder and first shogun of the Tokugawa shogunate of Japan, which ruled from 1603 until the Meiji Restoration in 1868. He was the third of the three "Great Unifiers" of Japan, along with his former lord Oda Nobunaga and fellow Oda subordinate Toyotomi Hideyoshi. The son of a minor daimyo, Ieyasu once lived as a hostage under daimyo Imagawa Yoshimoto on behalf of his father. He later succeeded as daimyo after his father's death, serving as ally, vassal, and general of the Oda clan, and building up his strength under Oda Nobunaga.

datapoints.description: Held hostage at the Honshōji Temple in Nagoya by the Oda clan.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Taken as a hostage to Sunpu after the Oda-Imagawa negotiation.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Briefly visited Okazaki to pay respects to his father's tomb and receive homage from retainers.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Returned to Mikawa Province to fight battles against the Oda clan on behalf of the Imagawa.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Fought his first battle at the siege of Terabe.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Led an operation to capture the stronghold of Marune.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Stopped by the Mizuno clan's forces while traveling back from Odaka.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Attacked and captured Kaminogō castle from the Udono clan.

sources.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Tokugawa Ieyasu

datapoints.description: Lived his final years at Sunpu Castle where he eventually died.
